subject: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0 — modeDescriptions implementation

validation_experiments.name: Typecheck
validation_experiments.detail: bun typecheck in packages/opencode shows 30+ errors. ZERO originate from modeDescriptions code (registry.ts describeTool, the 5 tool exports). New test (registry.test.ts line 564+) has no type errors of its own. Pre-existing errors: Layer.mergeAll arg limit (21 > 20), brokenPluginLayer missing Interface properties, campaign module Layer mismatches.

validation_experiments.name: Source code correctness — describeTool dispatch
validation_experiments.detail: describeTool at registry.ts:709 has exactly 3 dispatch cases: (1) TaskTool.id → describeTask(agent), (2) SkillTool.id → describeSkill(agent), (3) modeDescriptions Map lookup. Null guard at line 714: `if (!variants) return ""`. logWarning at line 720 fires when `variants[agent.name]` is undefined. DynamicDescription Phase 1 comment at lines 706-708.

validation_experiments.name: modeDescriptions exports — TypeScript validity
validation_experiments.detail: All 5 tools with modeDescriptions use valid `export const modeDescriptions = { ... } as const` syntax. plan.ts: architect, architecture-reviewer. coordinate.ts: orchestrator, general-man-agent. smart_delegate.ts: orchestrator, general-man-agent. read_source.ts: explorer, cartographer, surveyor. fragment.ts: surgeon, scalpel. No type errors in any export.

validation_experiments.name: Integration test structural correctness
validation_experiments.detail: Test at registry.test.ts:564-617 creates .opencode/tools/test-mode.ts dynamically with modeDescriptions {build, plan}, then calls registry.tools() for build and plan agents, verifying BUILD-SPECIFIC and PLAN-SPECIFIC in descriptions. Tests full chain: dynamic import → modeDescriptions capture → describeTool → description assembly. Cannot execute due to pre-existing layer gaps (TestInstance, Storage, EventStore missing from layer stack).

validation_experiments.name: Unintended file changes (git diff audit)
validation_experiments.detail: Phase 0 tool changes are clean: +5 lines coordinate.ts, +6 read_source.ts, +5 smart_delegate.ts (all modeDescriptions). roadmap.ts +2/-1 is unrelated deprecated-alias fix. registry.ts +44/-? adds modeDescriptions Map, describeTool, ToolGraph integration. No corrupted or unintended files. Diff includes 33 total files — remaining are other lanes (campaign, desktop, analytics tracking).
validation_experiments.evidence: git diff --stat shows 33 files; 4 .opencode/tools/ modified; only 3 for modeDescriptions

validation_experiments.name: Backward compatibility
validation_experiments.detail: Tools without modeDescriptions: describeTool returns "" (line 714 null guard), filtered by `.filter(Boolean)` at line 760, static description used as-is. Tools with modeDescriptions but unknown agent: logWarning + return "", static description used as-is. 27 of 32 tool files lack modeDescriptions — safe fallback confirmed.

additional_checks.name: Production path exercised
additional_checks.detail: describeTool called at registry.ts:758 inside tools() method (Interface.tools), which is the main production path for generating tool definitions sent to the LLM. Agent context flows from input.agent parameter. Full chain: load → modeDescriptions.set(namespace) → tools() → describeTool(toolId, agent) → description assembly → LLM tool list.

additional_checks.name: Type safety: modeDescriptions capture guard
additional_checks.detail: Guard at registry.ts:414: `mod.modeDescriptions !== null && typeof mod.modeDescriptions === 'object'`. Correctly rejects null, undefined, string, number, boolean, function. Falsely accepts arrays (typeof [] === 'object'), which would cause runtime error on `variants[agent.name]`. Low risk: as const enforces object literal.

additional_checks.name: DynamicDescription forward declaration
additional_checks.detail: Defined at tool.ts:16. Referenced only in comment at registry.ts:706-708. Zero executable references. Correct for Phase 0 — ready for Phase 1 wiring.

defects.title: namespace vs toolId key mismatch fragility
defects.description: modeDescriptions Map keyed by namespace (filename). describeTool looks up by toolId. Default exports match (toolId===namespace). Named exports would diverge (toolId=namespace_id). Silent failure: no mode description for named-export tools. Currently safe — all tools use default exports.
defects.recommendation: Use namespace consistently in describeTool lookup. Before Phase 1: ensure key scheme handles named exports or document the constraint.

defects.title: Test file cannot be typechecked or executed
defects.description: Pre-existing type errors prevent the file from typechecking. New modeDescriptions test at line 564 cannot be independently verified. Not caused by Phase 0.
defects.recommendation: Fix Layer.mergeAll (split into chained merges), fix brokenPluginLayer Interface, then re-run modeDescriptions test.

defects.title: Implementation claim inflation
defects.description: Claimed 5 .opencode/tools modified for modeDescriptions. Actually 3 modified in this diff (coordinate, read_source, smart_delegate). plan.ts and fragment.ts were pre-existing. roadmap.ts change is unrelated.
defects.recommendation: Correct publication record to state 3 tool files added modeDescriptions in this diff, 2 were pre-existing.

defects.title: 27 of 32 tool files lack modeDescriptions
defects.description: Only 5 of 32 tool files have modeDescriptions. Consistent with Phase 0 narrow scope. roadmap.ts (an orchestration tool modified in this diff) would benefit from modeDescriptions.
defects.recommendation: Phase 1 scope: add modeDescriptions to remaining tools, prioritizing orchestration and lifecycle tools.

admission_rationale: The implementation is functionally correct: describeTool dispatches correctly, logWarning fires on misses, backward compatibility is preserved, and the production path exercises the full chain. No type errors originate from the modeDescriptions code. Defects D1 (namespace-vs-toolId fragility) and D2 (unverifiable test) are real but non-blocking for Phase 0 — D1 is safe with current default-exports-only usage, D2 is pre-existing. The test is structurally sound and would pass if the layer stack were fixed. Recommend: admit the candidate boundary with a repair note for D1 before Phase 1, and fix pre-existing test layers to enable verification.
verified_boundary: modeDescriptions Map + describeTool dispatch + 5 tool exports + backward compat
consumer_purpose: Provide agent-mode-specific tool descriptions at LLM prompt construction time
